description: exact scope match, both bare (write) — grant equals require
prefix subsumption: parent scope grant covers child require
deep prefix subsumption: grandparent covers grandchild
child grant does NOT subsume parent require (subsumption is one-directional)
segment-boundary discipline: 'run' must not subsume 'runner' (no partial-segment match)
segment-boundary discipline: 'run' must not subsume 'running.task'
sibling scopes never match
unrelated top-level scopes never match
mode lattice: bare grant is write, satisfies a read require on the same scope
mode lattice: explicit write grant satisfies read require (write ⊒ read)
mode lattice: explicit write grant satisfies write require
mode lattice: write grant satisfies bare (write) require
mode lattice breakage proof — read grant does NOT satisfy write require (the write→read direction must not reverse)
mode lattice breakage proof — read grant does NOT satisfy bare (write) require
mode lattice: read grant satisfies read require on the same scope
mode lattice: read grant satisfies read require on a subsumed child scope
mode + scope composed: write grant on parent satisfies read require on child
mode + scope composed: write grant on parent satisfies write require on child
mode + scope composed: read grant on parent does NOT satisfy write require on child (mode axis still gates)
broad bare-prefix grant on a top-level scope sweeps the whole subtree
single-segment scope with hyphen: exact match (hyphen stays within a segment)
single-segment scope with hyphen: no subsumption across the hyphen boundary
dotted scope with hyphenated segments: parent subsumes child across the dot
read-mode broad grant covers a deeply nested read require
write-mode broad grant on parent confers at least read on child subtree
mode prefix does not leak into scope: write:run does not subsume runner
identical deep scopes with matching read mode
deep require with no ancestor in the grant set partial — single grant must cover both axes
granted SET: existential — a second grant in the set satisfies the require
granted SET: one grant subsumes the require by prefix and mode
granted SET: no cross-grant composition — no single grant covers both axes, so deny
granted SET: empty set denies (vacuous any)
granted SET: single broad grant subsumes the require
